japanese: 痩蛙 負けるな一茶 これにあり
romaji: Yasegaeru / makeru na Issa / kore ni ari
english: Skinny frog, don't give up! Issa is here with you.
notes: Humorous encouragement and compassion in Issa's voice.

japanese:
夏草や
兵どもが
夢の跡
romaji: Natsukusa ya / tsuwamono domo ga / yume no ato
english: Summer grasses; all that remains of warriors' dreams.
notes: Reflects impermanence through a historical battlefield image.

japanese:
古池や
蛙飛び込む
水の音
romaji: Furu ike ya / kawazu tobikomu / mizu no oto
english: The old pond; a frog jumps in — the sound of water.
notes: The most famous haiku worldwide, capturing sudden movement within eternal stillness.

japanese:
菜の花や
月は東に
日は西に
romaji: Na no hana ya / tsuki wa higashi ni / hi wa nishi ni
english: Mustard flowers; the moon in the east, the sun in the west.
notes: A breathtaking landscape painting in words, capturing a perfect moment at twilight.

japanese:
枯枝に
烏のとまりけり
秋の暮
romaji: Kare eda ni / karasu no tomari keri / aki de no kure
english: On a withered branch, a crow has settled — autumn nightfall.
notes: Evokes a powerful mood of monochrome bleakness and quiet contemplation.

japanese:
これがまあ
終の栖か
雪五尺
romaji: Kore ga maa / tsui no sumika ka / yuki go-shaku
english: Is this, then, my final dwelling place? Five feet of snow.
notes: Reflects acceptance and resilience in the face of harsh conditions and loss.

japanese:
痩蛙
負けるな一茶
これにあり
romaji: Yasegaeru / makeru na Issa / kore ni ari
english: Skinny frog, don't give up! Issa is here with you.
notes: Humorous encouragement and compassion in Issa's voice.

japanese: 五月雨を集めて早し最上川
romaji: Samidare o / atsume te hayashi / Mogamigawa
english: Gathering summer rains, the Mogami River rushes on.
notes: A dynamic river image with strong seasonal presence.

japanese:
しばらくは
花の上なる
月夜かな
romaji: Shibaraku wa / hana no ue naru / tsukiyo kana
english: For a while, moonlight rests above the blossoms.
notes: A quiet scene balancing moonlight and cherry blossoms.

japanese:
秋深き
隣は何を
する人ぞ
romaji: Aki fukaki / tonari wa nani o / suru hito zo
english: In deep autumn, what kind of person lives next door?
notes: Evokes solitude and curiosity.

japanese:
春の海
ひねもすのたり
のたりかな
romaji: Haru no umi / hinemosu notari / notari kana
english: The spring sea stretches lazily all day, rising and falling.
notes: Known for musical phrasing and calm motion.

japanese:
名月や
池をめぐりて
夜もすがら
romaji: Meigetsu ya / ike o megurite / yo mo sugara
english: The harvest moon; I wander around the pond all night long.
notes: Captures Basho's admiration for the autumn moon and the quiet beauty of nighttime reflection.

japanese:
柿食へば
鐘が鳴るなり
法隆寺
romaji: Kaki kueba / kane ga naru nari / Horyuji
english: I bite into a persimmon and a bell resounds—Horyuji Temple.
notes: A quintessential autumn haiku contrasting the everyday act of eating fruit with the timeless ringing of a temple bell.

japanese:
秋深き
隣は何を
する人ぞ
romaji: Aki fukaki / tonari wa nani o / suru hito zo
english: In deep autumn, what kind of person lives next door?
notes: Evokes solitude and curiosity.

japanese: 秋深き隣は何をする人ぞ
romaji: Aki fukaki / tonari wa nani o / suru hito zo
english: In deep autumn, what kind of person lives next door?
notes: Evokes solitude and curiosity.

japanese:
春の海
ひねもすのたり
のたりかな
romaji: Haru no umi / hinemosu notari / notari kana
english: The spring sea stretches lazily all day, rising and falling.
notes: Known for musical phrasing and calm motion.

japanese:
閑さや
岩にしみ入る
蝉の声
romaji: Shizukasa ya / iwa ni shimiiru / semi no koe
english: Such stillness — the cries of the cicadas sink into the rocks.
notes: Written at Yamadera temple; one of Basho's most celebrated haiku, evoking profound silence pierced by sound.

japanese:
秋深き
隣は何を
する人ぞ
romaji: Aki fukaki / tonari wa nani o / suru hito zo
english: In deep autumn, what kind of person lives next door?
notes: Evokes solitude and curiosity.

japanese:
痩蛙
負けるな一茶
これにあり
romaji: Yasegaeru / makeru na Issa / kore ni ari
english: Skinny frog, don't give up! Issa is here with you.
notes: Humorous encouragement and compassion in Issa's voice.

japanese:
春の海
ひねもすのたり
のたりかな
romaji: Haru no umi / hinemosu notari / notari kana
english: The spring sea stretches lazily all day, rising and falling.
notes: Known for musical phrasing and calm motion.
